title: Elder Mahā Kassapa (continued): Returning from alms round, Kassapa climbs the rock, Sits in meditation, without clinging, free from fear and terror. Returning from alms…
content:
Elder Mahā Kassapa (continued):

Returning from alms round, Kassapa climbs the rock,
Sits in meditation, without clinging, free from fear and terror.

Returning from alms round, Kassapa climbs the rock,
Sits in meditation, without clinging, burned, cooled.

Returning from alms round, Kassapa climbs the rock,
Sits in meditation, without clinging, duty done, without taints.

Enough for me, mindful, ardent, desiring to meditate.
Enough for me, a bhikkhu, ardent, desiring benefit.

Enough for me, a bhikkhu, ardent, desiring happiness.
Enough for me, a bhikkhu, ardent, practicing yoga.

title: - Great King, even wealthy nobles, wealthy brahmins, wealthy householders, rich, with great wealth and property, with abundant gold and silver, abundant goods and grains…
content:
- Great King, even wealthy nobles, wealthy brahmins, wealthy householders, rich, with great wealth and property, with abundant gold and silver, abundant goods and grains — it is for the sake of sensual pleasures, because of sensual pleasures, on account of sensual pleasures, that they consciously speak falsehood. Therefore, they will undergo long-term harm and suffering.

People are intoxicated
By wealth and by sensual desires,
They are greedy, deluded
In the sensual pleasures of the world,
Not clearly aware,
Excessively infatuated,
Like a fish that does not see the trap set,
Later they suffer,
Experiencing the result of evil deeds.

title: Then the Venerable Samiddhi, knowing it was Māra, spoke this verse to Māra: With faith, I left home to homelessness, My mindfulness and wisdom grow,…
content:
Then the Venerable Samiddhi, knowing it was Māra, spoke this verse to Māra:
With faith,
I left home to homelessness,
My mindfulness and wisdom grow,
My mind is concentrated.
Whatever form you create,
It does not frighten me.

title: The accumulation of bones from a single eon Would be a mountain as high, So said the Great Teacher. That mountain is said to be…
content:
The accumulation of bones from a single eon
Would be a mountain as high,
So said the Great Teacher.

That mountain is said to be
As great as Mount Vepulla,
North of Vulture's Peak
In the Magadhan hills.

But one who sees the Four Noble Truths
With perfect wisdom,
Suffering and its origin,
Will transcend all suffering;
The Noble Eightfold Path
Leads to suffering's stilling.

That person will wander on
At most seven more lives.
He has made an end of suffering,
Destroyed all fetters.

title: Bhikkhus, I do not see any other class of beings as diverse as the mind. Even the animals, bhikkhus, are diverse because of the mind.…
content: Bhikkhus, I do not see any other class of beings as diverse as the mind. Even the animals, bhikkhus, are diverse because of the mind. Therefore, bhikkhus, the mind is even more diverse than those animals. Therefore, bhikkhus, one should always contemplate one's own mind thus: 'For a long time, this mind has been defiled by greed, hatred, and delusion. Through the mind's defilement, beings are defiled; through the mind's purification, beings are purified.'

title: Then the brahmin Mānaṭṭhadda, after sitting on his own seat, spoke this verse to the Blessed One: Toward whom should one not be proud? Whom…
content:
Then the brahmin Mānaṭṭhadda, after sitting on his own seat, spoke this verse to the Blessed One:
Toward whom should one not be proud?
Whom should one respect?
Whom should one revere?
Whom should one honor as good?

( Blessed One ):
Toward mother and father,
Toward an elder brother,
Toward a teacher as the fourth,
One should not be proud,
One should respect them,
One should revere them,
One should honor them as good.
The Arahants,
Cool, taints destroyed,
Done what had to be done —
Subdue your pride before them,
That unsurpassed one,
You should indeed pay homage.

title: Ānanda: Going forth, I praise it as the Dhamma-eye. After examining, the Lord approved the going forth. Bondage is the household life, gathering dust; Freedom…
content:
Ānanda:
Going forth, I praise it as the Dhamma-eye.
After examining, the Lord approved the going forth.

Bondage is the household life, gathering dust;
Freedom is the homeless life; seeing this, he went forth.

After going forth, he avoided evil bodily deeds,
Abandoned evil speech, and purified his livelihood.

title: Elder Kosalavihāri: With faith, I went forth, living homeless. In the forest, a hut was built for me. I live heedfully, ardent, mindful, clearly comprehending.
content:
Elder Kosalavihāri:

With faith, I went forth, living homeless.
In the forest, a hut was built for me.
I live heedfully, ardent, mindful, clearly comprehending.

title: Here, bhikkhus, in whatever assembly, when the discourses spoken by the Tathāgata, deep, profound, transcendent, connected with the void, are being taught, the bhikkhus do…
content: Here, bhikkhus, in whatever assembly, when the discourses spoken by the Tathāgata, deep, profound, transcendent, connected with the void, are being taught, the bhikkhus do not listen well, do not lend an ear, do not apply their minds to understand them, and they do not think that these teachings should be learned and memorized. But when the poetic discourses composed by poets, with beautiful vowels and consonants, of outsiders, spoken by disciples of outsiders, are being taught, they listen well, lend an ear, apply their minds to understand them, and they think that these teachings should be learned and memorized. This, bhikkhus, is called an assembly trained in display, not trained in questioning.

title: Bhikkhus, there are these four perversions of perception, perversions of mind, perversions of view. What four? Regarding the impermanent as permanent, bhikkhus, that is a…
content: Bhikkhus, there are these four perversions of perception, perversions of mind, perversions of view. What four? Regarding the impermanent as permanent, bhikkhus, that is a perversion of perception, perversion of mind, perversion of view. Regarding the painful as pleasurable, bhikkhus, that is a perversion of perception, perversion of mind, perversion of view. Regarding the non-self as self, bhikkhus, that is a perversion of perception, perversion of mind, perversion of view. Regarding the impure as pure, bhikkhus, that is a perversion of perception, perversion of mind, perversion of view.
